summary: Linked the common fighter barrel object by changing `ftCo_Barrel.c` from NonMatching to Matching and tightening related source/header structure. The PR extracted repeated barrel knockback damage-field setup into a shared inline helper, moved the fake `SmallerHitCapsule` helper struct into `ftCommon/types.h`, and adjusted the GALE01 split sdata end address for `ftCo_Barrel.c`.
what_changed: `config/GALE01/splits.txt` changed the `ftCo_Barrel.c` `.sdata` range end from `0x804D3CA8` to `0x804D3CA5`.

`ftCo_Barrel.c` includes `ftCommon/inlines.h` and `ftCommon/types.h`, declares a local `struct SmallerHitCapsule hit`, calls `lbColl_80008D30((HitCapsule*) &hit, arg3)`, and replaces open-coded knockback setup with `ftCo_Barrel_ApplyKnockback(fp, kb_angle, (HitCapsule*) &hit)`.

`ftCo_BarrelWait.c` includes `ftCommon/inlines.h`, removes a file-local fake `SmallerHitCapsule` definition, simplifies `ftCo_8009EC70` by hoisting `Fighter* fp = GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` and early position/collision/camera setup, removes an explicit `PAD_STACK(4)`, and reuses `ftCo_Barrel_ApplyKnockback` before applying `Fighter_UnkTakeDamage_8006CC30(fp, hit.damage)`.

`ftCommon/inlines.h` adds includes for `placeholder.h`, `ft/ftcoll.h`, and `ftCommon/types.h`, changes `ftCo_SpawnEf` from a dummy `u8 _[8] = { 0 };` stack pad to `FORCE_PAD_STACK_8`, and adds the new static inline `ftCo_Barrel_ApplyKnockback`.
`ftCommon/types.h` gains `struct SmallerHitCapsule`, explicitly marked as fake with `@todo Fake, need to find real size of #HitCapsule`.
smart_moves: Factored the duplicated knockback-angle normalization and fighter damage bookkeeping into `ftCo_Barrel_ApplyKnockback`, reducing source drift between `ftCo_Barrel.c` and `ftCo_BarrelWait.c` while preserving the same field writes.
Kept the temporary reduced hit capsule representation explicit as `struct SmallerHitCapsule` and documented that it is fake, avoiding a misleading claim that the full `HitCapsule` layout is known.
Moved `SmallerHitCapsule` from a single C file into `ftCommon/types.h` so both barrel-related translation units can use the same layout workaround.
Used casts to `HitCapsule*` only at call boundaries (`lbColl_80008D30`, `ftCo_Barrel_ApplyKnockback`), making the matching workaround localized and searchable.
Replaced local dummy array stack padding in `ftCo_SpawnEf` with the named `FORCE_PAD_STACK_8` macro, making the intent of the matching artifact clearer.
decomp_lessons: When two nonmatching or near-matching functions share identical field writes, extracting the common sequence into a static inline can improve matching and reduce duplicated brittle code.
For incomplete large structs such as `HitCapsule`, a temporary smaller struct can be used for stack layout matching, but it should be named and annotated as fake until the real layout is known.
Header placement matters: a fake or partial struct needed across multiple ftCommon files belongs in a shared ftCommon header rather than as a file-local duplicate.
Prefer project stack-padding macros such as `FORCE_PAD_STACK_8` over anonymous dummy arrays when padding is needed for matching.
A PR that links a source file should include both the build-config transition to `Matching` and any required split-address adjustment.
naming_conventions: Common fighter barrel helper was named with the subsystem prefix and action context: `ftCo_Barrel_ApplyKnockback`.
Temporary partial layout type is named `SmallerHitCapsule`, reflecting that it is not the full `HitCapsule` and is a matching workaround.
Existing fighter field naming remains evidence-grounded and offset-like where unknown: `x1848_kb_angle`, `x184c_damaged_hurtbox`, `x1854_collpos`, `x1860_element`, `x21EC`.
The header guard style in `ftCommon/inlines.h` remains `MELEE_FT_CHARA_FTCOMMON_INLINES_H`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed `ftCo_Barrel.c` from nonmatching to matching in the configure object list.
Adjusted `.sdata` split end for `ftCo_Barrel.c` from `0x804D3CA8` to `0x804D3CA5`, indicating data boundary cleanup was part of linking.
Used a static inline helper for repeated knockback logic instead of duplicating code in multiple C files.
Used `FORCE_PAD_STACK_8` in `ftCo_SpawnEf` to express stack-shape padding explicitly.
Removed a `PAD_STACK(4)` from `ftCo_8009EC70` after refactoring the local variable and helper call structure.
Used `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` consistently for `Fighter* fp` initialization in the touched functions.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR had one issue comment according to counts, but no comment body was included in the provided excerpt, so no reviewer intent can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace `SmallerHitCapsule` with the real `HitCapsule` layout.
Search for other barrel or collision damage functions duplicating `ftCo_Barrel_ApplyKnockback` logic.
Check whether additional files still use dummy stack arrays instead of `FORCE_PAD_STACK_8` or related padding macros.
Look for subsequent changes to the `ftCo_Barrel.c` `.sdata` split range.
